title: Discovery: brand default stream paths, auto-find stream, saved credentials
description: Dan (2026-07-11): scan finds devices but adding is not working / not intuitive. 1) Always offer brand default RTSP/snapshot address templates (Reolink, Hikvision, Dahua, etc.) in preview, not gated on a detected brand; infer likely brand from port signature (554/443/8000 = Reolink). 2) Auto-find: server tries the common paths with the given creds and returns the first working main/sub pair. 3) Saved credentials: named credential sets managed in settings, applicable when probing/previewing/adding cameras; passwords never echoed.
close_reason: Find stream auto-detection, brand default paths, saved credentials, full-width discovery layout; deployed to Pi

title: Settings page: responsive PC-width layout and tabular scan results
description: Dan (2026-07-11): 1) Settings should use the width of a PC browser (multi-column, responsive). 2) Network scan results become a table: IP, manufacturer, protocols supported, ports, Add button, Preview button with protocol selection and credential prompt. Needs a pre-save preview proxy endpoint (SSRF fail-closed).
close_reason: Desktop two-column settings layout, scan results table with preview flow, preview endpoint; deployed to Pi and verified

title: Milestone 1: integration pass, CI image publish, release 0.1.0
description: End-to-end verification of the assembled app, GHCR publish workflow (image name pinned to ghcr.io/syracuse3dprintingorg/glancecam), CHANGELOG for 0.1.0, README finalization.
notes: Integration pass done 2026-07-11: 138 tests green, live smoke of all pages/APIs, password lifecycle verified, publish workflow added. Remaining: confirm first GHCR publish succeeds and the package is set Public, then tag v0.1.0 after on-device Pi 4 testing with Dan (SSH access promised).

title: Milestone 1: resource gating and stream budget
description: services/resources.py real budget math: hardware class detection (Pi3/4/5/x86), pixel-rate based live tile limit, per-surface budget (kiosk vs remote browser client hints), user-forward recommendations shown in UI.

title: Milestone 1: Pi native install + kiosk (cage/Chromium, rotation)
description: install.sh native mode: /opt/glancecam venv, go2rtc binary + systemd units, optional kiosk service gated on display presence, compositor rotation via wlr-randr, loopback-gated kiosk JS behavior.

title: Milestone 1: web UI (grid, fullscreen toggle, settings, discovery UX)
description: Minimalist dark grid that auto-scales tiles to fill the screen, click-to-fullscreen with main-channel swap (global+per-camera option), paused snapshot tiles beyond budget, settings pages with optional password, discovery wizard UX.

title: Milestone 1: core app skeleton (FastAPI + go2rtc + camera store)
description: Core service per docs/design/milestone-1.md: config with hardened save, statefile, netguard SSRF guard, camera store, go2rtc client+proxy, base routers/templates, compose files, repo docs. Built by wave-1 agent.
close_reason: Core skeleton built, 58 tests green, committed and pushed as e98cf7a

title: Milestone 1: discovery suite (LAN scan, ONVIF, Reolink, Home Assistant)
description: services/discovery/* and routers/discovery.py per design doc: job-based scan endpoints, brand-tagged LAN probe, ONVIF WS-Discovery + GetStreamUri, Reolink channel enumeration with main/sub, HA camera.* listing. One-click add proposals.

title: Codec-aware playback fallback for H265-only mains
description: Browsers mostly cannot decode H265 over WebRTC (and MSE support depends on hardware). When a camera's main stream is H265 and the surface cannot play it, fullscreen should stay on the (usually H264) sub stream with a quality note, or use a go2rtc ffmpeg transcode variant on capable hosts. Detect via MediaSource.isTypeSupported and the probed codec stored on the camera.

title: Vendor Bootstrap locally for internet-less LANs
description: base.html loads Bootstrap 5 from a CDN; a camera wall on an isolated LAN has no internet. Vendor the CSS/JS into static/vendor and drop the CDN reference.

title: CI smoke test for install.sh native mode
description: No automated test exercises install.sh end to end. Add a container-based CI job that runs the native-mode installer (FORCE_PI/FORCE_DISPLAY hooks exist) far enough to validate package steps, unit files, and go2rtc download logic.

title: Milestone 2: mDNS hostname glance.local with collision suffixing
description: Avahi hostname for the Pi install, appending -2/-3 when taken, mirroring PantryRaider's approach.

title: Milestone 2: touch calibration for Pi displays
description: LIBINPUT_CALIBRATION_MATRIX provisioning like PantryRaider firstboot; M1 relies on libinput defaults.

title: Milestone 2: Home Assistant event camera pop-ups
description: HA automation posts person/motion events; the grid auto-pops the matching camera fullscreen. Ring-buffer polling pattern from PantryRaider ha_events.

title: Milestone 2: Stream Deck camera engine
description: Simplified PantryRaider deck engine for cameras only: drag-and-drop editor retained, each key shows its camera thumbnail refreshing about every 1s, one press opens that camera full stream on the display.

title: Milestone 2: audio support in fullscreen view
description: go2rtc can pass audio via WebRTC; expose an unmute control in fullscreen only.

title: Phase 2: remote access (currently LAN only)
description: Design decision deferred by Dan: remote access lands in phase 2. Do not start without direction.
